task_completion.description: The agent loads the brain MRI scan into 3D Slicer.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface is visible with a brain MRI loaded in the Red (axial), Yellow (sagittal), and Green (coronal) slice views.

task_completion.description: The agent measures the two perpendicular diameters (A and B) on the axial slice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the Red (axial) slice view, two measurement lines are drawn across the bright tumor region. The lines appear roughly perpendicular to each other.

task_completion.description: The agent measures the craniocaudal extent (C) on the sagittal or coronal slice.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the Yellow (sagittal) or Green (coronal) slice view, a single vertical measurement line is drawn spanning the height of the tumor.

task_completion.description: The agent views the Markups module to verify measurement values.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Markups' module panel is open in the sidebar, showing a list of at least 3 control points or lines with length values visible in millimeters.

task_completion.description: The agent displays the content of the generated report file.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output (e.g., 'cat') displays the content of 'volume_estimate.txt'. The text includes A, B, and C diameter values and a calculated volume result.

task_completion.description: The agent verifies that all required output files have been saved.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A file explorer window or terminal listing ('ls') shows 'measurement_A.mrk.json', 'measurement_B.mrk.json', 'measurement_C.mrk.json', and 'volume_estimate.txt' in the specified directory.

integrity.description: The agent must use the Slicer GUI Markups tool for measurements.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the mouse cursor interacting with the Slicer toolbar (specifically the Ruler/Line icon) and drawing on the canvas, rather than solely using Python console commands to generate markup files.

integrity.description: The report values must be derived from the active session.
integrity.visual_evidence: The values shown in the 'Markups' panel in Slicer match the values written to the 'volume_estimate.txt' report. The agent does not simply copy-paste pre-existing numbers.

integrity.description: The markup files should be generated by Slicer.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent saves the markups via the Slicer 'Save Data' dialog or 'Export' function, rather than manually typing JSON content into a text file via the terminal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The tumor appears as a bright, enhancing (white) region on the T1-contrast MRI. The ABC/2 method requires: A=longest axial diameter, B=perpendicular axial diameter, C=craniocaudal (vertical) extent. Volume formula: (A*B*C)/2000 mL. Typical tumor diameters are 10-80mm. The lines drawn by the agent should span the visible tumor boundaries.
